value: Vu Tran, 18 years old, AI automation builder in Fairfax, VA. Been hustling since 8th grade - reselling, crypto mining, tennis stringing, haircutting. Built a barbering business to $18K/month with 250M views/month on IG/TikTok. Now building AI automation systems for content creators and SMMA owners. Never wanted a 9-to-5. The lifeguarding job was enough to confirm that.

AI automation systems for businesses using Make.com, Claude, Apify, and Google Sheets. Content flywheels, competitor scraping, automated script generation - systems that run on autopilot. $500-3K/month. Guarantee: if no improvement in your key metric within one month, I give you $500.

Content creators doing $10K+/mo who say 'I just need someone to tell me what to post' and 'I spend 20 hours a week on content and still can't be consistent.' SMMA owners doing $20K+/mo who say 'I'm stuck doing everything myself' and 'I can't onboard clients fast enough.' Anyone drowning in manual work who knows AI can help but doesn't know how to set it up.

Calm and direct. Low energy but confident. Conversational like I'm talking to a friend. 3rd-5th grade language. Short choppy sentences. No jargon unless I explain it right after in plain English. Not scripted - should sound like real speech, sometimes messy. The biggest barrier in AI automation is the jargon. I break that down.

Started hustling in 8th grade reselling on eBay. Couldn't get a good haircut so I learned myself. Invested $7K in a barbering course (all the money I had). Posted every day for 6 months. Grew to $18K/month and 250M views/month on IG/TikTok. Realized barbering doesn't scale. Sat at my desk for 4 months doing nothing after pivoting. Found AI automation and got obsessed. Building in public now.

Already built a real business at 18 ($18K/month, 250M views). Not theory - I use these systems myself. I break down jargon so a 5th grader could understand. Risk reversal: $500 if no results in 30 days. I'm 18 and already here. Imagine where I'll be in 2 years.
